group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1494790108377-be9c29b29330?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Mia Collins","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Mia Collins"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Workspace Designer"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","8 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"April 26, 2026"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-6 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"The closet-office trend is having a moment for a reason — you get a real, closeable workspace without sacrificing a bedroom. Here's how to plan, build, and finish one in a weekend."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 mb-8 p-3 rounded-lg bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Affiliate disclosure:"}]," This post may contain affiliate links to products we recommend. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"what-is-a-cloffice-and-why-everyone-s-building-one","children":"What Is a Cloffice (and Why Everyone's Building One)"}],"$L29","$L2a","$L2b","$L2c","$L2d","$L2e","$L2f","$L30","$L31","$L32","$L33","$L34","$L35","$L36","$L37","$L38","$L39","$L3a","$L3b","$L3c","$L3d","$L3e","$L3f","$L40","$L41","$L42","$L43","$L44","$L45","$L46","$L47","$L48","$L49","$L4a","$L4b","$L4c","$L4d","$L4e","$L4f","$L50","$L51","$L52","$L53","$L54","$L55","$L56","$L57","$L58","$L59","$L5a","$L5b","$L5c","$L5d","$L5e"]}],"$L5f","$L60","$L61"]}],"$L62"]}]]}]
63:I[94097,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
64:I[24170,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
65:I[55132,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1484788984921-03950022c9ef?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1506126613408-eca07ce68773?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
29:["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A \"cloffice\" — closet office — is a fully functional workspace built into a small closet, walk-in, or under-stair nook. The trend exploded during 2020 to 2021 when people started working from home permanently and discovered there was no spare room to dedicate. By 2026 the cloffice is its own design category, and Pinterest is overflowing with tiny doorless or sliding-door workspaces tucked into former coat closets."}}]
2a:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The appeal: you get a real, dedicated, <strong>closeable</strong> office without sacrificing a bedroom or eating the dining table. When the workday ends, you close the doors and the room becomes a regular bedroom or hallway again. The psychological separation between work and life — the thing remote workers struggle with most — gets baked into the architecture."}}]
2b:["$","h2","3",{"id":"will-your-closet-actually-work","children":"Will Your Closet Actually Work?"}]
2c:["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Three closet types convert well, one doesn't:"}}]
2d:["$","ul","5",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Walk-in closet</strong> (best) — interior space large enough to sit inside. Aim for 4'×4' minimum."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Reach-in closet</strong> (also good) — standard bedroom closet, 2'×4' or larger. Desk gets installed where the hanging rod was."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Under-stair closet</strong> (workable) — irregular but cozy, charming when finished."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Pantry / hall coat closet under 2' deep</strong> (don't bother) — too shallow for a real desk."}}]]}]
2e:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Measure the depth (front to back), width, and height. If depth is at least 22\" and width at least 36\", you have enough room for a real workspace."}}]
2f:["$","h2","7",{"id":"step-1-empty-it-completely","children":"Step 1: Empty It Completely"}]
30:["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Take everything out. Patch any holes from old hooks or hanging rods. If you're committing, repaint — a slightly darker or warmer color than the surrounding room makes the cloffice feel intentional rather than improvised."}}]
31:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Color picks that read \"cozy office\":"}}]
32:["$","ul","10",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Forest green</strong> — book-room aesthetic"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Warm terracotta</strong> — earthy, cottage-friendly"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Deep navy</strong> — scholarly, masculine-leaning"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Soft cream</strong> — keeps small space bright"}}]]}]
33:["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Avoid stark white — too sterile. Avoid black — turns the closet into a cave."}}]
34:["$","h2","12",{"id":"step-2-the-desk-solution","children":"Step 2: The Desk Solution"}]
35:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The desk is the hardest decision because most off-the-shelf desks are too deep (24\"+) for a closet. Four options that work:"}}]
36:["$","h3","14",{"id":"custom-cut-wood-plank","children":"Custom-cut wood plank"}]
37:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The most popular cloffice solution: a wood plank cut to the closet's exact width, mounted on heavy-duty L-brackets at desk height (29\" from floor). Hardware stores cut to size for ~$5. A 60\"×18\" plank in oak or pine runs $30 to $80."}}]
38:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This option looks the best, fits exactly, and lets you choose the wood species."}}]
39:["$","h3","17",{"id":"ikea-hack-alex-or-linnmon-cut-down","children":"IKEA hack: ALEX or LINNMON cut down"}]
3a:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The IKEA LINNMON tabletop ($25 to $50) can be cut down with a circular saw to fit any closet width. ALEX drawer units make great desk legs and add storage."}}]
3b:["$","h3","19",{"id":"floating-shelf-as-desk","children":"Floating shelf as desk"}]
3c:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A simple floating shelf 18\"×30\" or 18\"×48\", rated for desk weight (50 lb minimum), works as a minimal desk surface. No legs, no clutter underneath. Best for laptop-only setups."}}]
3d:["$","h3","21",{"id":"fold-down-wall-mounted-desk","children":"Fold-down / wall-mounted desk"}]
3e:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Murphy-style fold-down desks let you reclaim floor space when work is done. Pricier ($150 to $400) and more permanent installation. Worth it in studios and very small homes."}}]
3f:["$","h2","23",{"id":"step-3-the-chair-problem","children":"Step 3: The Chair Problem"}]
40:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Standard office chairs are too big for most cloffices. Options:"}}]
41:["$","ul","25",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Task stool</strong> — backless, adjusts up and down, fits anywhere ($60 to $200)"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Compact ergonomic chair</strong> — IKEA MARKUS in its smaller form factor, or HÅG Capisco"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Folding chair</strong> — works if the cloffice is occasional-use only"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Standing-only setup</strong> — set the desk at standing height, skip the chair entirely"}}]]}]
42:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Sit in your chair option for an hour before committing. Closet ergonomics get bad fast — you can't lean back, you can't sprawl. Make sure the chair won't ruin your back over time. For full ergonomic principles, see our <a href=\"/blog/ergonomic-home-office-setup-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">ergonomic home office setup guide</a>."}}]
43:["$","h2","27",{"id":"step-4-lighting-critical","children":"Step 4: Lighting (Critical)"}]
44:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most closets have no windows. You'll need to bring light to the cloffice or it will feel like a cave."}}]
45:["$","p","29",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Layered approach:"}}]
46:["$","ul","30",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Overhead light</strong> — replace any existing single bulb with a brighter LED (3000K to 3500K, 800+ lumens). If there's no fixture, install one or use a battery-powered LED puck light on the ceiling."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Task light on desk</strong> — desk lamp or <a href=\"/blog/monitor-light-bar-vs-desk-lamp\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">monitor light bar</a> for direct work lighting."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Accent light</strong> — small wall sconce or LED strip along the back of the desk for ambient warmth."}}]]}]
47:["$","p","31",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Skip fluorescent lighting. The greenish cast in a small space is brutal."}}]
48:["$","h2","32",{"id":"step-5-storage-above-and-below","children":"Step 5: Storage Above and Below"}]
49:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The advantage of cloffices: vertical space is abundant. Use it."}}]
4a:["$","ul","34",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Above the desk</strong> — floating shelves at 12\", 24\", 36\" above the desk surface for books, supplies, decor. Or a small pegboard (see our <a href=\"/blog/pegboard-wall-organization-small-home-office\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">pegboard wall organization ideas</a>)."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Below the desk</strong> — a 2-drawer file cabinet, baskets for cables, a small trash bin"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Behind the desk</strong> — a corkboard, calendar, or magnetic whiteboard makes the back wall functional"}}]]}]
4b:["$","h2","35",{"id":"step-6-the-aesthetic-finish","children":"Step 6: The Aesthetic Finish"}]
4c:["$","p","36",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The cloffice's small footprint means details show. Touches that matter:"}}]
4d:["$","ul","37",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Wallpaper or wall paneling</strong> — a single accent wall behind the desk transforms the space"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>A small art piece</strong> — eye-level, framed, intentional"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>One plant</strong> — a small trailing pothos on a top shelf, or a sculptural snake plant on the floor"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Quality desk objects</strong> — a leather mousepad, a wooden pencil cup, a ceramic coaster — small choices that read as curated"}}]]}]
4e:["$","p","38",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For more ideas in this direction, see our <a href=\"/blog/aesthetic-desk-setup-ideas-cozy-home-office\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">aesthetic desk setup guide</a>."}}]
4f:["$","h2","39",{"id":"door-question-open-closed-or-removed","children":"Door Question: Open, Closed, or Removed?"}]
50:["$","p","40",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Three approaches:"}}]
51:["$","ul","41",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Keep the doors</strong> — close them at the end of the day. Best for the work-life separation argument."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Remove the doors</strong> — open the cloffice as a built-in alcove. Visually expands the room. Best when the closet is in a less-trafficked area."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Replace with curtain</strong> — fabric curtain on a tension rod, pulled closed when needed. Best of both — visually softer, easy to install, fully reversible for renters."}}]]}]
52:["$","h2","42",{"id":"common-cloffice-mistakes","children":"Common Cloffice Mistakes"}]
53:["$","ul","43",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Forcing a too-deep desk</strong> — measure twice, cut once."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Ignoring ventilation</strong> — closets get warm with a person and a laptop. Crack the door, add a small clip-on fan."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Skipping cable management</strong> — small space + tangled cables looks chaotic immediately. Use cable clips and a single power strip mounted under the desk."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Buying a regular office chair</strong> — they don't fit. See above."}}]]}]
54:["$","h2","44",{"id":"frequently-asked-questions","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}]
55:["$","p","45",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>How much does a cloffice cost to build?</strong>"}}]
56:["$","p","46",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$$200 to $500 for a budget DIY (custom plank desk, basic chair, lights). $1,000 to $2,500 for a polished setup with custom millwork, premium lighting, and curated decor."}}]
57:["$","p","47",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Will a cloffice add value to my home?</strong>"}}]
58:["$","p","48",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Real estate agents in major markets confirm it does — particularly for homes marketed to remote workers. A clean, finished cloffice photographs well on listings and is a differentiator over comparable homes."}}]
59:["$","p","49",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Do I need to ventilate a cloffice?</strong>"}}]
5a:["$","p","50",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For 1 to 2 hours a day, the existing closet air is fine. For 8-hour daily use, leave the door cracked or install a small fan. A laptop adds about 50W of heat, plus your body adds another 100W — that builds up."}}]
5b:["$","p","51",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Can I hide my work at the end of the day?</strong>"}}]
5c:["$","p","52",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Yes — that's the point. Close the doors (or curtain), and the cloffice disappears into the rest of the room. This is the killer feature most home offices don't have."}}]
5d:["$","p","53",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Is a cloffice good for video calls?</strong>"}}]
5e:["$","p","54",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Surprisingly, yes — the small enclosed space dampens echo dramatically, making audio sound great with even cheap mics. Lighting is the only challenge (see Step 4 above)."}}]
5f:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Workspace","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-blue-50 text-blue-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Workspace"}],[["$","$L7","small-space",{"href":"/blog/tag/small-space","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 transition-colors","children":["#","small-space"]}],["$","$L7","remote-work",{"href":"/blog/tag/remote-work","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 transition-colors","children":["#","remote-work"]}],["$","$L7","diy",{"href":"/blog/tag/diy","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","diy"]}],["$","$L7","cloffice",{"href":"/blog/tag/cloffice","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","cloffice"]}],["$","$L7","home-office",{"href":"/blog/tag/home-office","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 60:["$","$L63",null,{"title":"Cloffice Setup: How to Build a Tiny Home Office in a Closet","slug":"cloffice-setup-tiny-home-office-closet"}]
61:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L64",null,{}]}]
62:["$","aside",null,{"className":"space-y-5 print:hidden","children":[["$","$L65",null,{"items":[{"id":"what-is-a-cloffice-and-why-everyone-s-building-one","text":"What Is a Cloffice (and Why Everyone's Building One)","level":2},{"id":"will-your-closet-actually-work","text":"Will Your Closet Actually Work?","level":2},{"id":"step-1-empty-it-completely","text":"Step 1: Empty It 66:["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors line-clamp-2 leading-snug","children":"How to Soundproof a Home Office: A Renter-Friendly Guide for 2026"}]
67:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-stone-950 rounded-2xl p-6 text-center","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white text-lg mb-2","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Enjoy this article?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400 mb-4","children":"Get more workspace ideas in your inbox."}],["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/contact","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-semibold px-4 py-2.5 rounded-xl text-white bg-orange-500 hover:bg-orange-600 transition-colors","children":["Get in touch ",["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":"$a:props:children:0:props:children:1:0:props:children:1:props:children:0:props:children:props:children:1:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-arrow-up-right","size":14}]]}]]}]
23:[["$","meta","0",{"charSet":"utf-8"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"viewport","content":"width=device-width, initial-scale=1"}]]
68:I[27201,["/_next/static/chunks/01xlw8hd842-c.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js"],"IconMark"]

25:[["$","title","0",{"children":"Cloffice Setup: How to Build a Tiny Home Office in a Closet"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"description","content":"The closet-office trend is having a moment for a reason — you get a real, closeable workspace without sacrificing a bedroom.
